steps.title: Target the explanation
steps.content: The proposed explanation is that people mentally handle electronic funds differently from physical money, which could lead them to buy a different array of foods. To weaken it, the evidence should show electronic-transfer recipients quickly ended up using physical money too.

steps.title: Remove the money-form difference
steps.content: A supports a behavioral difference. C concerns purchase frequency, not how the funds were conceived or allocated. D only varies payment schedules within the physical-transfer group. If electronic recipients withdrew the whole transfer as cash, both groups spent physical money, so the proposed money-form difference cannot explain the food difference. The answer is B.
